description: Conformance test vectors for draft-xkumakichi-xaip-receipts-03 (wire formatVersion "1"). Every hash, canonical payload, and signature in this file is real and reproducible. Run `node check.mjs` (Node >= 18, no dependencies) to re-derive every value from this file and verify all signatures; the checker fails on any drift.

keys.note: TEST keys only, generated for these vectors. Never reuse for anything. DIDs are illustrative; verification here uses the embedded SPKI keys directly (DID resolution is out of band).

preimageVectors.name: string_raw_utf8
preimageVectors.description: Text values hash their raw UTF-8 content bytes (5 bytes for 'hello'), NOT the JSON-string serialization. Formats that hash the JSON form ('"hello"', 7 bytes) produce the wrongHash value below; profile 1 deliberately hashes content bytes so the hash of a text value equals the hash of the same bytes delivered as raw binary.

preimageVectors.name: unicode_string_raw_utf8
preimageVectors.description: Non-ASCII text hashes its raw UTF-8 bytes with no Unicode normalization applied.

preimageVectors.name: json_key_order_invariance
preimageVectors.description: Structured JSON values hash the UTF-8 bytes of their JCS (RFC 8785) canonical form, so member order cannot change the hash. This value reproduces in independent RFC 8785 implementations.

preimageVectors.name: json_object_task
preimageVectors.description: The example receipt's task input. JCS sorts members lexicographically before hashing.

preimageVectors.name: empty_input_sentinel
preimageVectors.description: An absent input or output (null, undefined, or no output at all — e.g. a failed call) hashes the empty byte string. This sentinel is the REQUIRED resultHash for formatVersion 1 failure receipts that commit to no output.

payloadVectors.name: v1_canonical_payload
payloadVectors.description: Canonical payload of a formatVersion 1 receipt: the nine base fields plus formatVersion, JCS-serialized (formatVersion sorts between failureType and latencyMs). signature/callerSignature/toolMetadata are never included.

payloadVectors.name: toolmetadata_excluded_from_payload
payloadVectors.description: toolMetadata is unsigned hint data (draft §3.1/§6): a receipt carrying it has a canonical payload byte-identical to the same receipt without it.

payloadVectors.name: legacy_payload_nine_fields
payloadVectors.description: Legacy receipts (no formatVersion) canonicalize the nine base fields only. Verifiers reconstruct the payload from the fields exactly as received.

receiptVectors.name: v1_cosigned_valid
receiptVectors.description: A complete, verifiable formatVersion 1 receipt (the §2.1 example of the draft). Both signatures verify against the embedded test keys over the v1_canonical_payload bytes.

receiptVectors.name: v1_failure_sentinel
receiptVectors.description: A failure receipt: success=false, failureType from the registry, resultHash = the empty-input sentinel (no output existed). Executor signature only.

receiptVectors.name: legacy_agent_only
receiptVectors.description: A legacy receipt (no formatVersion) remains verifiable under the nine-field payload rule.

receiptVectors.name: tampered_success_flip
receiptVectors.description: Tamper detection: the v1_cosigned_valid receipt with success flipped to false. Both signatures MUST fail verification.

rejectionVectors.name: v1_truncated_hash
rejectionVectors.description: formatVersion 1 requires taskHash/resultHash to be exactly 64 lowercase hex chars. A 64-bit truncation is collision-findable (~2^32 work) and MUST be rejected fail-closed, regardless of signature validity.
rejectionVectors.mustReject: taskHash is not 64 lowercase hex characters

rejectionVectors.name: v1_uppercase_hash
rejectionVectors.description: Hex case changes bytes: uppercase hashes violate the lowercase MUST and are rejected fail-closed.
rejectionVectors.mustReject: taskHash is not 64 lowercase hex characters

rejectionVectors.name: v1_failuretype_inconsistent
rejectionVectors.description: failureType MUST be "" exactly when success is true. A successful receipt carrying a failure type is rejected fail-closed.
rejectionVectors.mustReject: failureType must be "" when success is true
